heading: Step 2: Define the policy in the Cedar policy language.
body:
Specify the rules for restricting resources using [Cedar policy language](https://docs.cedarpolicy.com/). You can copy
and modify the following example policies for your organization:

- [Restrict Number of Clusters](tf-restrict-number-of-clusters)

- [Restrict Cloud Provider](tf-restrict-cloud-provider)

- [Restrict Region](tf-restrict-region)

- [Restrict Cloud Provider and Region](tf-restrict-provider-region)

- [Restrict IP Addresses](tf-restrict-ip-addresses)

- [Enforce Disk Size (GB)](tf-limit-max-disk-size)

- [Enforce Cluster Topology (Replica Set, Sharded, or Global Cluster)](tf-restrict-cluster-type)

- [Enforce Minimum Shard Count](tf-restrict-shard-count)

- [Enforce Dedicated Config Servers for Sharded Clusters](tf-enforce-config-server-management-mode)

> **Note:** We recommend creating multiple, simple Atlas Resource Policies to make tracking easier.
> For example, if you want to restrict cloud provider and multiple regions,
> consider creating one Atlas Resource Policy that restricts the cloud provider and another
> Atlas Resource Policy that restricts the regions.
